mcqs.question: What is the span of a set of vectors?

options.text: The set of all possible linear combinations of those vectors
The set of all scalar multiples of those vectors
The set of all linearly independent vectors
The set of all orthogonal vectors

mcqs.explanation: The span of a set of vectors is the set of all possible linear combinations of those vectors.

mcqs.question: Can the span of a set of vectors be a subspace?

options.text: Yes, if it is closed under addition and scalar multiplication
No, it cannot be a subspace
Yes, if it is closed under addition but not scalar multiplication
Yes, if it is closed under scalar multiplication but not addition

mcqs.explanation: The span of a set of vectors can be a subspace if it is closed under addition and scalar multiplication.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a linear combination and a span?

options.text: A linear combination is an expression of a vector as the sum of scalar multiples of other vectors, while a span is the set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors
A linear combination is the set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors, while a span is an expression of a vector as the sum of scalar multiples of other vectors
A linear combination is a scalar multiple of a vector, while a span is the set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors
A linear combination is the set of all scalar multiples of a vector, while a span is the set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors

mcqs.explanation: A linear combination is an expression of a vector as the sum of scalar multiples of other vectors, while a span is the set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors.

mcqs.question: Can a set of vectors have more than one span?

options.text: Yes, a set of vectors can have more than one span
No, a set of vectors can have only one span
Yes, if the set of vectors is linearly independent
Yes, if the set of vectors is linearly dependent

mcqs.explanation: A set of vectors can have only one span, which is the set of all possible linear combinations of those vectors.

mcqs.question: What is the span of a single vector?

options.text: The set of all scalar multiples of that vector
The set of all linear combinations of that vector
The set of all orthogonal vectors to that vector
The set of all linearly independent vectors to that vector

mcqs.explanation: The span of a single vector is the set of all scalar multiples of that vector.

options.text: Yes, the span of a set of vectors can be empty
No, the span of a set of vectors cannot be empty
Yes, if the set of vectors is linearly independent
Yes, if the set of vectors is linearly dependent

mcqs.explanation: The span of a set of vectors cannot be empty, because it always contains the zero vector.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship between the span of a set of vectors and the linear independence of those vectors?

options.text: If a set of vectors is linearly independent, then their span is a subspace
If a set of vectors is linearly dependent, then their span is a subspace
If the span of a set of vectors is a subspace, then those vectors are linearly independent
If the span of a set of vectors is a subspace, then those vectors are linearly dependent

mcqs.question: Can the span of a set of vectors be used to solve systems of linear equations?

options.text: Yes, the span of a set of vectors can be used to solve systems of linear equations

mcqs.question: What is the geometric interpretation of the span of a set of vectors?

options.text: The set of all points that can be reached by linear combinations of the vectors
The set of all points that cannot be reached by linear combinations of the vectors
The set of all points that can be reached by scalar multiples of the vectors
The set of all points that cannot be reached by scalar multiples of the vectors

mcqs.explanation: The geometric interpretation of the span of a set of vectors is the set of all points that can be reached by linear combinations of the vectors.

mcqs.question: What is the span of a set of vectors in R^2?

options.text: A line or a plane
A line or a point
A plane or a point
A line or a space

mcqs.explanation: The span of a set of vectors in R^2 is a line or a plane.
